description: Subscribable resources for GitHub. An agent with the github-app integration can call subscribe_to_events with any of these resource_types to receive future webhook events on a specific resource.

github_issue.description: Issue in a GitHub repository. Receives issue lifecycle and comment events.

github_pull_request.description: Pull request in a GitHub repository. Receives the key PR lifecycle signals, human review/comment activity, and a single CI rollup (check_suite.completed) — intentionally narrow so agents aren't woken 20+ times per CI run on a single PR. If you need finer-grained signals, fetch via the github integration on demand.

github_discussion.description: Discussion thread in a GitHub repository. Receives discussion lifecycle and comment events.

github_release.description: Release (tag) in a GitHub repository. Receives release lifecycle events.

github_commit.description: Events tied to a specific commit SHA. Use when no PR is linked (e.g. direct push to main). Both 7-char short SHAs and 40-char full SHAs are accepted — pick one and stick with it for stable routing. Only the CI rollup (check_suite.completed) fires per CI cycle — not per-individual-check — to cut webhook noise.

github_branch.description: Events on a specific branch: pushes, branch creation, branch deletion.

github_repository.description: Repository-wide events: forks, stars, watchers. Use sparingly on popular repos — high event volume.
